Pakistani strikes kill 36 civilians in Afghanistan -- Pakistani forces' ground operations and airstrikes killed at least **36 civilians** in Afghanistan overnight and wounded
Peru's Fujimori wins presidency by slim margin -- Peru's conservative Keiko Fujimori has won the 7 June presidential runoff, defeating left-wing candidate Roberto Sanchez

An explosion from an explosive device seriously injured three people, including a Ukrainian construction tycoon and his family, at a residential building in Monaco on Monday night; the suspect fled on foot into neighbouring France and was identified via video surveillance.
WhatsApp is rolling out usernames globally to its three billion account holders over the next few months, letting users connect without sharing phone numbers, with names limited to 35 characters.

A new report from Ramp and Revelio Labs found that companies spending heavily on AI - defined as an average of $30 per employee per month - saw headcount increase 10.2%, including a 12% rise in entry-level roles, though the authors caution the findings do not show AI universally creates jobs.

Offenders in England and Wales who kill a current or ex-partner will face a new minimum sentence of 25 years - an increase of 10 years - under plans announced by justice secretary David Lammy following a seven-year campaign by victims' mothers.

Funerals were held in Lahore on Wednesday for 14 schoolchildren killed when the roof of a tutoring centre collapsed on Tuesday, with at least two people including the building owner arrested as investigators determined negligence by the owner and construction workers appears to have caused the collapse.
